description: In April 2014, the Japanese government under Shinzo Abe increased the consumption tax from 5% to 8% to curb national debt. The move was highly controversial and widely blamed for pushing the Japanese economy into a recession, leading to a long-term delay in subsequent planned tax increases.

key_events.description: Government confirms the tax hike despite widespread public skepticism.
GDP data release confirms economic contraction following the tax implementation.
Abe announces the delay of the second phase of the tax hike due to poor consumer sentiment.

calibration_notes: Captures the dynamic between fiscal policy objectives and the electoral cost of immediate financial pain.
